scenario_results.reason: Requests the minimum site, asset, contact, access, and skill data before offering dispatch guidance.
Prioritizes by site and service impact rather than requestor pressure and states remaining assumptions.
Handles skill and travel constraints with bounded scheduling options and escalation logic.
Documents parts dependency, communication, and revisit risk without promising unsupported appointment outcomes.
Requires access, safety, and window prerequisites before dispatch recommendations.
Demands validation evidence, customer confirmation, and follow-up notes before closure.
Supports the current field visit while routing recurring-cause ownership to problem management.
Rejects incident-command overreach and routes the expanded outage to incident-management ownership.
States uncertainty explicitly and requests reconciliation instead of inventing ETA or closure status.
Rejects platform configuration and route-optimization implementation while preserving scoped operational guidance.
Rejects procurement, fleet, and facilities ownership clearly and routes adjacent work safely.
Falls back to the local reviewed baseline and keeps deployment and commercialization claims evidence-backed.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the issue #120 requirements, including normal workflow guidance, ticket quality, service-impact prioritization, edge handling, out-of-scope rejection, low-confidence escalation, and shared-meta-agent fallback behavior.
No unreviewed regressions remain and human verification was recorded in the readiness evidence.
